/overview: The open protocol for machine-to-machine payments, built on an open IETF specification for HTTP 402
/faq: Common questions about the Machine Payments Protocol, payment methods, wallets, and how MPP compares to other solutions
/guides/building-with-an-llm: Give your LLM or AI agent the context it needs to build MPP integrations from your codebase
/quickstart: Get started with MPP in minutes using the TypeScript, Python, or Rust SDK
/quickstart/server: Charge for access to protected API resources using challenges, credentials, and receipts
/quickstart/agent: Connect your AI agent to MPP-enabled services and handle payments automatically
/quickstart/client: Handle payment-gated resources automatically in your frontend or backend application
/protocol: An extensible framework for standardizing HTTP 402 Payment Required for machine-to-machine payments
/protocol/http-402: How MPP uses the HTTP 402 status code to signal that payment is required before accessing a resource
/protocol/challenges: Server-issued payment requirements that describe the amount, currency, and accepted payment methods
/protocol/credentials: Client-submitted payment proofs that demonstrate a valid payment has been made for a request
/protocol/receipts: Server-issued acknowledgments that confirm successful payment and grant access to the resource
/protocol/transports: How MPP payment flows are carried over different transport protocols like HTTP and MCP
/protocol/transports/http: Payment flows using standard HTTP headers for challenges, credentials, and receipts
/protocol/transports/mcp: Payment flows for AI tool calls using MCP and JSON-RPC as the underlying transport
/payment-methods: Available payment methods in MPP and how to choose the right one for your use case
/intents/charge: Immediate one-time payments where the full amount is collected in a single request-response cycle
/payment-methods/tempo: Stablecoin payments on the Tempo blockchain with near-instant finality and low transaction costs
/payment-methods/tempo/charge: One-time TIP-20 stablecoin token transfers settled on-chain with cryptographic receipts
/payment-methods/tempo/session: Low-cost high-throughput streaming payments using off-chain state channels on Tempo
/payment-methods/stripe: Accept card, wallet, and bank transfer payments through Stripe's global payment infrastructure
/payment-methods/stripe/charge: One-time payments using Stripe Shared Payment Tokens for seamless card-based transactions
/payment-methods/card: Card payments via encrypted network tokens for direct card-present and card-not-present flows
/payment-methods/card/charge: One-time payments using encrypted network tokens for secure card-based transactions
/payment-methods/lightning: Bitcoin payments over the Lightning Network for instant, low-fee global transactions
/payment-methods/lightning/charge: One-time payments using BOLT11 invoices settled instantly over the Lightning Network
/payment-methods/lightning/session: Pay-as-you-go streaming payments over Lightning using incrementally settled micropayments
/payment-methods/custom: Build your own payment method by implementing the core challenge-credential-receipt control flow
/sdk: Official MPP SDK implementations in TypeScript, Python, and Rust for clients, servers, and agents
/sdk/typescript: The mppx TypeScript library for building MPP clients, servers, and middleware integrations
/sdk/typescript/core/Expires: Generate relative expiration timestamps for challenges, credentials, and payment requests
/sdk/typescript/cli: Built-in command-line tool for making paid HTTP requests and managing wallets from the terminal
/sdk/python: The pympp Python library for building MPP clients and servers in Python applications
/sdk/python/core: Challenge, Credential, and Receipt primitive types for working with the MPP protocol in Python
/sdk/python/client: Handle HTTP 402 responses automatically and manage payment flows in Python clients
/sdk/python/server: Protect API endpoints with payment requirements and verify credentials in Python servers
/sdk/rust: The mpp Rust library for high-performance MPP clients and servers with zero-copy parsing
/sdk/rust/core: Challenge, Credential, and Receipt primitive types for working with the MPP protocol in Rust
/sdk/rust/client: Handle HTTP 402 responses automatically and manage payment flows in Rust clients
/sdk/rust/server: Protect API endpoints with payment requirements and verify credentials in Rust servers
/brand: Download MPP logos, icons, and brand assets for use in your documentation and marketing materials
/services: Discover MPP-enabled services your agents and applications can pay for and use
/guides/one-time-payments: Accept single fixed-price payments for API calls using the charge intent with any payment method
/guides/pay-as-you-go: Accept metered usage-based payments using sessions that track consumption over time
/guides/streamed-payments: Accept continuous real-time payments that stream value as a service is consumed
/guides/multiple-payment-methods: Offer your users a choice of payment methods including stablecoins, cards, and Lightning
